summary: Elena Vasquez from Aegis Cloud Security support called Dana Moretti of Forge Industries to discuss the recovery and closure of support case 7537 related to a Detect platform outage. The outage began March 10th with approximately six hours of complete threat monitoring visibility loss caused by a single point of failure in the event ingestion layer, followed by degraded performance through March 15th. Aegis implemented architectural fixes including redundant processing nodes and a circuit breaker pattern, with full restoration confirmed March 18th. Dana needs documentation for her CISO, including a data recovery summary for the blind-spot window and a formal post-incident report.
actionItems: Elena Vasquez: Send Forge Industries a data recovery summary detailing what event data was and was not captured during the six-hour outage window, targeting Thursday but no later than Friday
Elena Vasquez: Share the finalized post-incident report (PIR/RCA) with Dana Moretti once it is out of draft
Elena Vasquez: Add tenant health confirmation metrics and timestamp to the case notes and keep case open until both documents are received and confirmed
Dana Moretti: Prepare CISO briefing using the root cause details, timeline, and remediation information provided, incorporating incoming documentation

keyMoments.text: Dana raises concern about a single point of failure in critical threat monitoring infrastructure, calling it 'not a small thing'
keyMoments.speaker: Dana Moretti

keyMoments.text: Elena confirms real architectural changes — redundant processing nodes and circuit breaker pattern — are live, not just a patch
keyMoments.speaker: Elena Vasquez

keyMoments.text: Elena acknowledges a gap in processed alert history during the outage window, noting raw data fidelity cannot be guaranteed
keyMoments.speaker: Elena Vasquez

keyMoments.text: Elena pulls live tenant metrics confirming Forge Industries is fully healthy — ingestion normal, latency under 2 seconds, no queue backlog
keyMoments.speaker: Elena Vasquez
